items.title: 'We've been looked after': Hantavirus cruise passengers out of quarantine

children.content: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

items.title: 'More bad news' expected as testing widens after second WA bird flu case

children.content: Producers across an enormous stretch of southern WA are facing a tense few days, as authorities investigate more than 50 dead birds found along the state's southern coast.

image.altText: A man with beanie on holding a chicken with chickens on ground

items.title: Egg shortage unlikely as poultry industry braces for deadly bird flu

children.content: The poultry industry says it is concerned but prepared as the deadly H5 avian influenza variant reaches Australian shores for the first time.

image.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

items.title: What we know about how H5N1 bird flu got here and where it may appear next

children.content: Authorities were worried about avian influenza coming in from the north, until the virus started moving across the Southern Ocean. Now infected birds have been found in Western Australia. Where else could birds carrying the virus land?

articles.title: Second Australian case of deadly bird flu confirmed

children.content: Authorities say a second bird found sick on WA's south coast had the deadly H5 strain of avian flu, as one of the nation's biggest chicken producers announces it is locking down its WA operations.

items.title: Fourth WA council in turmoil after three councillors quit in three days

children.content: Three councillors have quit the City of South Perth in three days, citing concerns over a rates hike and broader dysfunction within the council.

image.altText: Buildings with the colours of a sunset all around them.

items.title: 'No longer appropriate': WA MP calls to end Lord's Prayer in parliament

children.content: A Labor MP says he wants the Lord’s Prayer scrapped from WA parliament, despite the reading of the Christian prayer being a long-held tradition before sessions.

articles.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

image.altText: Lionel Messi waves to the crowd as he walks off the field after a game

children.content: Kylian Mbappe and France put the foot down against Iraq after inclement weather forces a two-hour delay, while Erling Haaland nets again as Norway takes on Senegal. Follow live.

updates.title: 90+2' Ismaila Sarr gets another in stoppage time
57' Erling Haaland strikes again for Norway!

items.title: Security agencies issue blunt 'act swiftly' warning on AI

children.content: The Five Eyes agencies warn leaders must "act swiftly" to address the imminent threat as AI increases the "speed, scale and sophistication of cyber threats".

image.altText: A row of men in suits sitting on a stage

items.title: WiseTech shares rise as chair denies human trafficking reports

children.content: WiseTech regains some ground after Monday's 18 per cent plunge. The ASX is seesawing between gains and losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The blurred side profile of a man walking past a screen that says "ASX welcomes WiseTech Global".

items.title: High cost of living fuels record regional migration, report says

children.content: The rate at which capital city dwellers are moving the regions is at its highest level on record, which  the report's author says shows the trend is more than a post-COVID blip.

image.altText: Aerial view of sailing boats on a water with a city as a backdrop

items.title: You won't see Hugh Jackman the same after The Death of Robin Hood

children.content: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

items.title: Is it possible to have a monoculture? This WA town doesn't think so

children.content: A Muslim leader from a regional WA town says he would like to have a conversation with Pauline Hanson about her ideology following her comments calling for monocultural Australia.

items.title: More or Less quiz: Which British PMs have lasted the longest?

children.content: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

image.altText: A screenshot from a Daily Star broadcast shows a lettuce with googly eyes next to portrait of Liz Truss

items.title: Inspired by volcanoes, this new technology could affect billions of people

children.content: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains, on which a billion people depend.

image.altText: the earth rotates and is surrounded by tiny reflective particles

items.title: Australians have record-low trust in the United States, new poll finds

children.content: The Lowy Institute's latest poll shows Australia's trust in the US has fallen to a record low.

image.altText: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump walk together along the Temple of Heaven.

items.title: Former US Federal Reserve chair Alan Greenspan dies aged 100

children.content: The wife of the former federal reserve chair says he died from complications of Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: Alan Greenman wearing a suit with a red tie and glasses while smiling.

items.title: Scientists 'blown away' by baby coral resilience on Ningaloo Reef

children.content: After suffering its worst bleaching event on record, only to be battered by Cyclone Narelle six months later, the World Heritage-listed reef is showing its first signs of recovery thanks to the efforts of marine scientists.

gallery.altText: Marine researchers goes underwater to place spawning cones to gather and preserve coral reefs.
Spawn cones deployed in the Bundegi Reef to collect baby corals in a bid to restore bleached corals in Ningaloo Reef.
Marine researchers and scientists from various organisations working at the Mesa Reef in Exmouth to restore the bleched corals

items.title: The hidden dynamic that could undermine global stocks

children.content: As we plough on through the fourth consecutive year of global stock records and huge annual advances, it's not surprising that many pundits have begun to question just how much longer this boom can continue.

items.title: Christian Brothers 'squib' on abuse compensation, lawyer says

children.content: The Catholic order says it will sell off its remaining assets to fund abuse compensation, but the money raised is expected to fall short of what is owed to victims.

items.title: WA football great Cousins taking inspiration from Daniher in MND fight

children.content: WA football great Bryan Cousins is taking inspiration from the late Neale Daniher in his fight with motor neurone disease, as he pays tribute to son Ben for his efforts in combating addiction.

items.title: Australian sprinter feels 'absolutely robbed' after Commonwealth Games snub

children.content: Josh Azzopardi opens up after missing out on selection as a 100 metres runner in Australia's athletics team for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

items.title: Veterans paid extra $7m in abuse compensation after review

children.content: The Defence Force Ombudsman made the extra payments after a federal court case forced it to admit its reparation scheme didn't consider the ongoing impact of assaults.

items.title: Rescuers cry as baby turtles embark on 1,400km journey

children.content: Two turtles are set to be flown 1,400 kilometres in a journey that could take them from Western Australia to Madagascar.

image.altText: Turtle

items.title: Police speech stuns descendants gathered at site of outback mass killing

children.content: Ronnie's great-grandfather collected bones and teeth from an outback creek bed. Now police say they're 'not proud' of what occurred.
